meta.scene_summary: We're busy building the future while the past is still holding our data hostage.

cooking.headline: OpenAI is making its own chips because buying them was getting too expensive
cooking.summary: OpenAI is moving into custom hardware, which is a classic move for anyone tired of paying their landlord's inflated rent. It turns out that when you burn through cash like a bonfire, eventually you decide to just build your own fireplace.
cooking.plain_english: OpenAI is partnering with Broadcom to design and manufacture its own custom computer chips to support its artificial intelligence infrastructure.

cooking.headline: Gemini can now use your computer, because clicking buttons was the only thing standing between us and Skynet.
cooking.summary: Google is teaching Gemini to navigate interfaces just like a human, which is great until it starts rage-clicking your terminal. It's essentially giving a LLM a mouse and keyboard to see if it can handle the chaos of a browser tab graveyard.
cooking.plain_english: Google has updated its AI model to perform tasks on a computer by interacting with the screen, mouse, and keyboard.

cooking.headline: Apple is raising prices because your wallet was looking too heavy
cooking.summary: Apple has announced a price hike across the Mac and iPad lineups, ensuring your bank account stays as slim as their new hardware. It is the tech equivalent of a landlord raising the rent just because they know you really like the wallpaper.
cooking.plain_english: Apple has increased the retail prices for several of its MacBook and iPad models.

cooking.headline: Cloudflare just made your login flow one less headache
cooking.summary: Cloudflare is letting you manage your own OAuth, which is great because nobody actually enjoys fighting with identity providers. It's like finally getting the master key to your own apartment instead of asking the landlord for permission every time you want to enter.
cooking.plain_english: Cloudflare has introduced a feature that allows users to manage their own OAuth authentication processes directly through the platform.

cooked.headline: LastPass is back at it again with another data breach
cooked.summary: At this point, LastPass notifying users of a data breach feels less like a security alert and more like a subscription service for bad news. It is the digital equivalent of a screen door on a submarine that just keeps letting the water in.
cooked.plain_english: The password management company LastPass has announced that it has experienced another security incident involving user data.

cooked.headline: Elastic is tightening its belt by 7%
cooked.summary: Elastic is laying off 7% of its workforce despite being the industry standard for finding literally anything in a database. It turns out that even the best search engine in the world couldn't find a way to avoid the current industry-wide layoff trend.
cooked.plain_english: The data search company Elastic is laying off 7% of its employees.

cooked.headline: Alibaba allegedly decided to 'clone' Claude instead of building it
cooked.summary: Anthropic is accusing Alibaba of scraping their model capabilities, which is basically the AI equivalent of Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V in a group project. You'd think a tech giant would try to pass the class on their own merit, but apparently, academic integrity didn't make it into the training data.
cooked.plain_english: Anthropic claims that Alibaba improperly accessed and used technology from its AI model, Claude.

cooked.headline: Ford realizes that firing the experts to save money actually just creates expensive, broken cars.
cooked.summary: Ford tried to replace veteran engineers with AI, only to find out that machines are terrible at passing down institutional knowledge. It turns out that 'move fast and break things' is a terrible strategy when the things you are breaking weigh two tons.
cooked.plain_english: Ford is rehiring hundreds of quality control engineers after AI tools failed to effectively manage vehicle production and training processes.

cooked.headline: Curl just found a bug older than most junior developers
cooked.summary: Security researchers uncovered six vulnerabilities in curl, including one that has been hiding in the codebase since the dawn of time. It is essentially finding a piece of expired milk in the back of the fridge that has achieved sentience.
cooked.plain_english: Researchers discovered six new security vulnerabilities in the data transfer tool curl, one of which has existed since the software's inception.

cooked.headline: Paying for the privilege of seeing ads
cooked.summary: OpenAI decided that paying for a subscription wasn't enough, so now they're throwing ads into the mix. It's the digital equivalent of paying your cable bill just to watch commercials for laundry detergent.
cooked.plain_english: OpenAI has begun displaying advertisements within its paid service offerings.
